info.title: Profile Services API
info.description: Generated from framework-free Route descriptors (Elysia + Bun runtime).

Email.description: Email address used for authentication and notifications. Trimmed and lowercased on submit.

Password.description: Password (8-128 chars). Must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character (@$!%*?&).

name.description: Display name (optional). Defaults to email handle.

acceptedTosVersion.description: Current TOS version the user has accepted (LGPD consent).

acceptedPrivacyVersion.description: Current privacy policy version the user has accepted (LGPD consent).

CreateAccountRequest.description: Sign-up payload. LGPD requires explicit `acceptedTosVersion` / `acceptedPrivacyVersion` matching the current published versions.

ConfirmAccountDeletionRequest.description: Step 2: confirm the emailed 6-digit code to permanently delete the account.

message.description: Localized confirmation that a code was sent.

cooldownSeconds.description: Seconds before a resend is allowed.

RequestAccountDeletionRequest.description: Step 1 of the code-confirmed account deletion. Validates the confirmation phrase + current password and emails a 6-digit code; nothing is deleted yet.

ShortDescription.example: A short description of the resource.

TwoFactorCode.description: 6-digit numeric TOTP code from the authenticator app.

message.description: Localized success message for the operation.

PasswordMessageResponse.description: Generic success envelope for password-management endpoints.

ForgotPasswordRequest.description: Forgot-password trigger. Always returns the same generic success message regardless of whether the email exists, to avoid account enumeration.

PasswordInput.description: Account password for authentication. Lenient validation (1-128 chars) to support legacy accounts; new passwords must satisfy the stricter PasswordSchema policy.

LoginRequest.description: Login payload. Password validation is lenient here (legacy accounts) — the strict policy applies on registration / change.

token.description: Single-use password-reset token sent via email.

newPassword.description: Password (8-128 chars). Must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character (@$!%*?&).

ResetPasswordRequest.description: Token-based password reset. The token is single-use and was previously emailed via the forgot-password flow.

minLength.description: Minimum password length enforced by the server.

maxLength.description: Maximum password length enforced by the server.

requireUppercase.description: Whether at least one uppercase letter is required.

requireLowercase.description: Whether at least one lowercase letter is required.

requireNumber.description: Whether at least one digit is required.

requireSpecialChar.description: Whether at least one special character is required.

specialChars.description: Allowed special characters for password validation.

PasswordPolicy.description: Password validation rules enforced by the server. Frontend forms (sign-up, password change, password-strength meter) consume this so the rules live in one place.

SocialUrl.description: Public HTTP(S) URL (max 500 chars). Trimmed on submit.

description.description: Long-form job description. Plaintext or markdown.

requirements.description: List of required qualifications (max 40 entries, 200 chars each).

skills.description: List of relevant skills (max 40 entries, 60 chars each).

paymentCurrency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the salary range. Optional.

minEnglishLevel.description: Minimum English proficiency level expected from candidates. Optional.

CreateJobRequest.description: Create-job payload. `applyUrl` is the public link recruiters share with candidates.

example.title: Senior Backend Engineer
example.description: We are looking for a senior backend engineer to join our team.

isActive.description: Whether the job is currently active and visible.

paymentCurrency.description: Updated ISO 4217 currency code. Null clears the previous value.

remotePolicy.description: Updated remote / hybrid / on-site policy. Null clears the previous value.

minEnglishLevel.description: Updated minimum English level. Null clears the previous value.

UpdateJobRequest.description: Partial update of a job. Nullable enums let users clear an existing selection.

resumeId.description: Resume id to apply with. Defaults to the primary resume.

ApplyToJobRequest.description: Submit a job application. `resumeId` references the resume the candidate wants to apply with; if omitted, the primary resume is used.

example.coverLetter: I am excited about this role because of your engineering culture.

ConfirmEmailChangeRequest.description: Step 2: confirm the emailed 6-digit code to apply the email change.

message.description: Localized confirmation that a code was sent.

cooldownSeconds.description: Seconds before a resend is allowed.

RequestEmailChangeRequest.description: Step 1 of the code-confirmed email change. Verifies the current password and emails a 6-digit code to the NEW address; the email is not changed yet.

newPassword.description: Password (8-128 chars). Must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character (@$!%*?&).

ChangePasswordRequest.description: Self-service password change. Requires the current password (lenient validation) and a new password meeting the strict policy.

ConfirmPasswordChangeRequest.description: Step 2: confirm the emailed 6-digit code to apply the password change.

message.description: Localized confirmation that a code was sent.

cooldownSeconds.description: Seconds before a resend is allowed.

newPassword.description: Password (8-128 chars). Must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character (@$!%*?&).

RequestPasswordChangeRequest.description: Step 1 of the code-confirmed password change. Validates current + new password and emails a 6-digit code; the password is not changed yet.

Username.description: Public handle (3-30 chars). Lowercase letters, digits, and single underscores only. Cannot start/end with `_`, contain `__`, or use a reserved name.

Phone.description: Phone number, free-form up to 20 characters. Recommended format: E.164 (e.g. `+5511999990000`).

LinkedInUrl.description: LinkedIn profile or company URL (e.g. `https://www.linkedin.com/in/<handle>` or `https://www.linkedin.com/company/<slug>`).

Bio.description: Optional long-form summary shown at the top of the resume.
Bio.example: Engineer interested in building robust systems.

title.description: Resume title shown in the user dashboard (max 100 chars).

isPublic.description: Whether the resume is publicly viewable via its slug.

fullName.description: Full name to render on the resume. Optional.

jobTitle.description: Target job title or current role shown under the name.

sections.description: Generic resume sections. Each item references a SectionType by key.

CreateResumeRequest.description: Create-resume payload. `sections` are generic — each item references a SectionType by key with content validated server-side against the SectionType definition.

example.title: Senior Software Engineer Resume
example.summary: Backend engineer with 8+ years of experience building distributed systems.

title.description: Resume title shown in the user dashboard (max 100 chars).

isPublic.description: Whether the resume is publicly viewable via its slug.

fullName.description: Full name to render on the resume. Optional.

jobTitle.description: Target job title or current role shown under the name.

sections.description: Generic resume sections. Each item references a SectionType by key.

UpdateResumeRequest.description: Partial update of a resume. Same shape as CreateResumeRequest with all fields optional.

title.description: Title for the new resume (max 100 chars).

styleId.description: ResumeStyle for the copy. Defaults to the source resume style.

language.description: Language of the copy (e.g. pt-br, en). Defaults to the source language.

itemIds.description: Item ids of this section to copy. Omitted = every item.

include.description: Sections (and optionally which items) to copy from the source. Omitted = copy everything.

DuplicateResumeRequest.description: Snapshot-copy of an existing resume. The copy diverges from the source — later edits to either do not affect the other. Publish state (slug/isPublic) and stats are reset.

FullName.description: Full name (2-100 characters). Trimmed on submit.

events.description: Event types this webhook subscribes to. At least one required.

CreateWebhookRequest.description: Register an outbound webhook. URL is fetched through the SafeFetchPort allowlist on delivery (SSRF defense).

events.description: Updated event subscriptions. Omit to leave unchanged.

enabled.description: Whether the webhook is currently active.

UpdateWebhookRequest.description: Partial update of a webhook (rotate URL, change event subscriptions, toggle enabled).

get.description: Static JSON document Apple fetches to associate this domain with the patchcareers iOS app. Served without a .json extension per Apple spec.
Static JSON statement list Google fetches to verify the patchcareers Android app owns this domain for App Links.

get.summary: Liveness probe — always 200 once the process is booted
Readiness probe — every backend must answer within 2s

get.description: Returns service metrics in Prometheus exposition format.

post.description: Registers a new user account. Authentication is established via httpOnly session cookie set in parallel; no bearer tokens are returned in the body.

delete.description: Deactivates the authenticated user account (soft delete).

post.description: Permanently erases the account after verifying the emailed 6-digit code.

Validates the confirmation phrase "DELETE MY ACCOUNT" AND the current password (re-authentication gate to prevent stolen-cookie deletes), then emails a 6-digit code. The account is only erased after POST /v1/accounts/delete/confirm.

get.summary: Counts of admin-actionable queues: reports, verifications, stale shadow profiles

post.description: Clears server-side caches and pushes an `invalidate` message through the SSE stream so every connected client refetches.

patch.summary: Toggle a flag or update its role restriction
patch.description: Turning ON is rejected with 409 when any ancestor is OFF. Deprecated flags are read-only.

get.summary: Preview transitive descendants affected when a flag is turned OFF
List every FitQuestion in the pool

delete.description: Disables 2FA and removes all backup codes. Requires re-authentication via the current password OR a valid TOTP code (either suffices).

post.description: Generates new backup codes, replacing existing ones. Shown only once.

post.summary: Setup two-factor authentication for the current user
post.description: Generates TOTP secret and QR code. 2FA is not enabled until verified.

get.description: Returns 2FA status including enabled state and backup codes remaining.

post.description: Verifies TOTP token and enables 2FA. Returns backup codes (shown only once).

get.description: Returns how many seconds the authenticated user must wait before requesting another verification email. The UI uses this so the countdown survives page reloads.

post.description: Sends a verification email to the authenticated user. No body required.
Verifies the user email using the token received via email.
Sends a password reset email if the account exists. Always returns success to prevent email enumeration.
Authenticates user with email and password. Sets the session cookie. Returns `{userId}` or `{userId, twoFactorRequired}` when 2FA is enabled.
Completes login by validating a TOTP or backup code. Sets the session cookie.
Logs out the user by invalidating refresh token(s) and clearing the session cookie.

get.summary: Whether a given OAuth provider is configured.

get.description: OAuth login endpoints. When `redirect_uri` was supplied at start and validates against the allowlist, redirects there instead of UI_BASE_URL.
OAuth login endpoint. Optional `redirect_uri` query param enables the mobile deep-link flow when present in OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI_ALLOWLIST.
OAuth login endpoints. When `redirect_uri` was supplied at start and validates against the allowlist, redirects there instead of UI_BASE_URL.
OAuth login endpoint. Optional `redirect_uri` query param enables the mobile deep-link flow when present in OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI_ALLOWLIST.
Frontend renders a button per provider and uses `window.location.href = startUrl` to begin the flow.

post.description: Rolls the session cookie. Body `refreshToken` is optional and used only by non-browser clients.
Resets the user password using a valid reset token received via email.

get.description: Returns current user data if authenticated via session cookie or JWT bearer.

post.summary: Exchange a one-shot sessionExchangeId for a token pair (mobile)
post.description: V2 D42 — native clients that opted out of cookies via `Accept-Mode: tokens` receive a one-shot `sessionExchangeId` in the login / verify-2fa response. This endpoint swaps that id for a real access/refresh token pair. The id is single-use (the second call returns 401) and expires 60 seconds after issuance.

get.summary: List active sessions (devices) for the current user.

delete.summary: Revoke a specific session (device) by refresh-token id.

post.summary: Submit tailored applications to every open job that matches minFit. Bounded by maxApplications (default 20, cap 100).

post.description: Accepts up to 100 events per request. Events are stored as-is; props is free-form JSON. The expected event names + shapes are listed by `/v1/events/schemas`.

get.description: Returns `{events:[{name,version,propsSchema,requiredContext?,piiFields?}]}`. The frontend uses this to know which event names + prop shapes are valid before emitting.

Active flags for the current user
get.description: Returns `{flags: Record<string, boolean>}`. A flag is `true` only if its own enabled state, every ancestor, and the role restriction all allow it. Frontend uses `flags[key]` directly without per-key resolution.
Same payload as /v1/feature-flags/active. Kept for transition.
Server-Sent Events channel emitting `invalidate` messages when admin toggles a flag or triggers a broadcast refresh. Clients should re-fetch /evaluate on each message.

get.description: Returns `{ locale, entries: { [EnumName]: { [VALUE]: label } } }`. Locale is chosen from `Accept-Language` (supports `en`, `pt-BR`).

get.description: Returns `{ locale, entries: { [CODE]: localized message } }`. Locale is chosen from `Accept-Language` (supports `en`, `pt-BR`).

get.summary: Notification-template dictionary in the negotiated locale
get.description: Returns `{ locale, entries: { [TYPE]: { title, body, params[] } } }`. Templates may contain `{ param }` placeholders — the client substitutes them at render time using the `params` list as the allowed key set.

get.description: Returns `{widgets:[{id,type,title,size,data,actions?,cta?}]}`. Adding/removing widgets is a backend-only change. Today the server emits widgets for greeting, counters, recent notifications and follow-ups; the frontend simply iterates `widgets[]` and dispatches on `type`.
Returns `{section, fields:[...], actions:[...], info:[...]}`. The frontend renders settings pages from this payload — adding a setting field or action is a backend-only change.

example.coverLetterTemplate:

usernameChangeAvailableAt.description: When the user may next change their username, or null if they can change it now. The first change after sign-up is always free; later changes are gated once every `cooldownDays` (see GET /v1/users/username/rules).

Get username format rules (single source of truth for client gating)
get.description: Returns the regex sources + min/max so the frontend can compile a matching RegExp and validate input without a round-trip per keystroke. The body never changes between requests of the same backend version, hence the long cache.

cooldownDays.description: Days a user must wait between username changes. The first change after sign-up is free.

post.summary: Validate username (multi-error breakdown for client forms)
post.description: Returns every format/availability problem at once. Each error carries a stable `code` plus a `message` already localized via the request `Accept-Language` header.
